metadata.archetype_justification: No database connections, no backend API, no persistent state, and no write operations detected. All data is served from in-memory mock services. The application is a pure frontend template with no server-side runtime.

findings.title: No Managed Compute
findings.description: No managed compute infrastructure. The application is deployed via rsync to a remote server. No ECS, EKS, Lambda, Fargate, or EC2 instances defined in IaC.
findings.gap: No cloud-native compute. Deployment is a raw file copy to an unmanaged server with no orchestration, scaling, or health management.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the Angular application and deploy to EKS (preferred) or use S3 + CloudFront for static hosting. Create a Dockerfile with multi-stage build (Node.js build → nginx serve) and deploy via Helm to EKS.

findings.title: No Network Security Configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network configuration exists. No IaC defines any network infrastructure.
findings.gap: No network security configuration. The deployment server's network posture is unknown and unmanaged from this repository.
findings.recommendation: When moving to AWS, deploy within a VPC with private subnets. Use CloudFront as CDN/entry point with WAF for DDoS protection. If using EKS, configure network policies and security groups via IaC.

findings.title: No API Entry Point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or any managed entry point. The application is served directly from the rsync target with no gateway, load balancer, or CDN.
findings.gap: No managed entry point. No throttling, no CDN caching, no WAF protection, no TLS termination management visible.
findings.recommendation: Deploy CloudFront as CDN with S3 origin for static assets, or use an ALB/Ingress in front of EKS-hosted containers. Configure WAF rules for web application protection.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. The application is deployed to a single remote server via rsync with no scaling capability.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling. The deployment cannot respond to traffic spikes or scale down during low demand.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to EKS, configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) based on CPU/request metrics. Alternatively, use CloudFront + S3 (inherently scalable for static content).

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No infrastructure-as-code exists in this repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize files were found.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. Infrastructure cannot be reproduced, versioned, or audited.
findings.recommendation: Define all target infrastructure in CDK or Terraform: VPC, CloudFront distribution, S3 bucket (if static hosting), or EKS cluster + Helm chart (if containerized).

findings.title: Partial CI/CD Automation
findings.description: CI/CD pipelines exist but are partial and fragmented: Travis CI (Node 10, lint + build only), GitHub Actions demoDeploy.yml (Node 12, build + rsync deploy with no testing stage).
findings.gap: No automated test stage in any deployment pipeline. No deployment to managed infrastructure. No rollback capability. Legacy Node.js versions in CI. Duplicated CI systems.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate to GitHub Actions. Create a proper pipeline: lint → test → build → deploy-staging → integration-test → deploy-prod. Use Node.js 18+ in CI. Deploy via Helm/ArgoCD to EKS.

findings.title: Compound Legacy Framework Stack
findings.description: TypeScript 4.9 with Angular 15 and Nebular 11. Multiple deprecated dependencies: node-sass, protractor, tslint, codelyzer. IE 11 still in browserslist.
findings.gap: Compound legacy: Angular 15 (2+ major versions behind), deprecated toolchain, stalled UI framework (Nebular), and IE 11 polyfills.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Angular to 17+ (standalone components, signals), TypeScript to 5.x, RxJS to 7.x. Replace node-sass with dart-sass, protractor with Playwright. Drop IE 11. Evaluate replacing Nebular.

findings.title: Monolithic Single Deployable
findings.description: Single deployable Angular monolith. All features bundled into one dist/ output and deployed as a unit via rsync.
findings.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith. Cannot independently deploy, scale, or evolve individual feature areas.
findings.recommendation: Containerize as-is for immediate cloud-native deployment. Consider Module Federation for micro-frontend decomposition when scale warrants. Add backend API layer as separate services.

findings.title: No API Versioning Strategy
findings.description: No API versioning strategy. The application has routing paths but no version prefixes, no API specification files.
findings.gap: No versioning framework. When backend APIs are added, there is no pattern for versioning in place.
findings.recommendation: When adding backend APIs, implement URL-path versioning (/api/v1/) from the start. Document the API with OpenAPI specification.

findings.title: No Cloud-Native Asset Storage
findings.description: Static assets stored in src/assets/ and bundled into the application at build time. No S3, no managed object storage, no CDN.
findings.gap: All unstructured data bundled with the application. No cloud-native storage, no CDN-served assets.
findings.recommendation: Move static assets to S3 with CloudFront CDN. This improves performance, reduces bundle size, and enables independent asset updates.

findings.title: No Real Data Access Layer
findings.description: The application uses mock data services only. The pattern (abstract interfaces + implementations) is reasonable but produces no actual data access.
findings.gap: No real data access exists. Mock layer only — no backend API integration.
findings.recommendation: Implement the existing abstract data classes with HTTP service implementations calling backend APIs. Use Angular HttpClient with centralized interceptor.

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail, no audit logging configuration, no IaC defining any logging infrastructure.
findings.gap: No audit logging. No record of user actions, deployment events, or infrastructure changes.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to AWS, enable CloudTrail for all AWS API calls. Configure CloudWatch Logs for application-level logging. Integrate CloudWatch RUM for frontend error tracking.

findings.title: Mock Authentication Only
findings.description: The application uses NbDummyAuthStrategy — a mock authentication strategy that accepts any credentials with a 3-second delay.
findings.gap: Authentication is entirely mocked. No real identity verification, no token-based auth, no session management.
findings.recommendation: Replace NbDummyAuthStrategy with Amazon Cognito for OAuth2/OIDC authentication. Configure JWT validation on all API endpoints.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Provider
findings.description: No centralized identity provider integration. Uses Nebular dummy auth strategy with no real identity verification.
findings.gap: No IdP integration. Cannot authenticate users against any real identity source. No SSO, no federated identity.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito for user pool management and OAuth2/OIDC flows. Enable SSO with corporate identity providers via SAML/OIDC federation.

findings.title: API Key Hardcoded in Source
findings.description: A Google Maps API key is hardcoded in src/app/app.module.ts. CI/CD secrets stored in GitHub Secrets without rotation.
findings.gap: API key exposed in source code. No AWS Secrets Manager or Vault usage. No rotation.
findings.recommendation: Remove the hardcoded Google Maps API key from source. Use environment variables injected at build/runtime. Store production secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with rotation.

findings.title: No Compute Hardening or Patching
findings.description: No compute hardening or patching strategy. No vulnerability scanning. Deprecated dependencies with known vulnerabilities remain in use.
findings.gap: No patching strategy. No vulnerability scanning. Deprecated packages (node-sass, protractor) with known vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Add dependency vulnerability scanning (npm audit, Snyk, Dependabot) to CI pipeline. When deploying to EKS, use hardened base images (Bottlerocket, distroless).

findings.title: Linting Only — No Security Scanning
findings.description: ESLint is configured and runs in CI. However, no SAST, no dependency vulnerability scanning, no container scanning exists.
findings.gap: No security-focused scanning. Linting provides code quality but not vulnerability detection.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot configuration for automated dependency updates. Add npm audit as a CI step. Consider Semgrep for SAST. Enable ECR image scanning when containerized.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: No distributed tracing instrumentation. No X-Ray, OpenTelemetry, or tracing SDK in the dependency manifest.
findings.gap: No tracing capability. No visibility into request flows when backend services are introduced.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with AWS X-Ray or OpenTelemetry when adding backend services. Add CloudWatch RUM for frontend performance tracking.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: No custom metrics published. AnalyticsService exists but has no CloudWatch or metrics integration.
findings.gap: No metrics beyond default hosting metrics. No visibility into application usage or feature adoption.
findings.recommendation: Integrate CloudWatch RUM for client-side performance. Publish custom CloudWatch metrics for business events when deployed to AWS.

findings.title: No Alerting or Anomaly Detection
findings.description: No alerting or anomaly detection configured. No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
findings.gap: No alerting. Issues will only be discovered through user reports.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms on error rates, latency (CloudFront), and availability. Enable anomaly detection. Integrate with SNS/PagerDuty for alerting.

findings.title: Direct-to-Production Deployment
findings.description: Direct-to-production deployment via rsync with --delete-after. No staged rollout, no canary, no blue/green, no health checks.
findings.gap: No safe deployment strategy. All users immediately affected by every deployment. No rollback capability.
findings.recommendation: Move to container-based deployment on EKS with canary deployments using Argo Rollouts. Or use CloudFront + S3 with percentage-based traffic shifting.

findings.title: No Tests
findings.description: No test files found. Test infrastructure (karma, jasmine, protractor) configured but no .spec.ts or e2e test files exist.
findings.gap: Zero test coverage. No unit tests, no integration tests, no e2e tests. Changes deployed without automated verification.
findings.recommendation: Add unit tests (Jest), integration tests, and E2E tests (Playwright/Cypress). Run tests in CI as a mandatory quality gate before deployment.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No incident response automation. No runbooks, no SSM Automation documents, no self-healing patterns.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common scenarios. When on EKS, configure liveness/readiness probes. Document incident escalation procedures.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership. No CODEOWNERS, no dashboards, no alarms with named owners.
findings.gap: No observability ownership structure. Monitoring is entirely absent.
findings.recommendation: Create per-service CloudWatch dashboards. Add CODEOWNERS file. Define alarm ownership and on-call rotation.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging
findings.description: No resource tagging. No AWS resources exist to tag — no IaC and no cloud infrastructure defined.
findings.gap: No tagging governance. When AWS infrastructure is created, no tagging standard exists.
findings.recommendation: When creating IaC, implement default_tags with mandatory tags: Environment, Team, Service, CostCenter. Configure Tag Policies for enforcement.

top_gaps.impact: Cannot reproduce environments, no disaster recovery, no automated provisioning

top_gaps.gap: Partial CI/CD with legacy tooling and rsync deploy
top_gaps.impact: Fragile deployments, no quality gates, no rollback

top_gaps.gap: rsync direct-to-production with no staged rollout
top_gaps.impact: All users hit changes immediately; no safe deployment

top_gaps.gap: Google Maps API key hardcoded in source
top_gaps.impact: Credential exposure risk; no rotation; keys in git history
Cannot independently scale or evolve features

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Containerize the monolith first, then selectively extract high-value features. Angular's lazy-loaded modules provide natural extraction boundaries.
decomposition_strategy.effort_signal: Low to Medium for containerization; Medium for selective extraction

triggering_questions.note: No managed compute — rsync to server

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 (<3), no Dockerfile or container definitions found

immediate_actions: Create Helm chart for EKS deployment

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected; DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures)

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures; all logic in application layer — threshold not met

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected in the repository. Application uses only in-memory mock data.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated (no persistent data store); has_persistent_data_store=false
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No persistent data store exists. INF-Q2 was not evaluated because the system has no database.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist; stateless-utility archetype
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. The application has no data processing responsibilities.

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context ('Angular admin dashboard template') does not contain AI-related signal terms.
